relationships.description: NSL Chapter 4 general security duties map to NIS2 risk management measures. Both establish a legal obligation for proportionate security measures based on risk assessment.
NSL Chapter 4 general security duties require systematic identification and risk assessment (§ 4-2), aligning with NSM GP Category 1 identification and mapping principles.

NSL § 4-5 Varslingsplikt (reporting duty) requires notification of security-threatening events. NSM GP 4.1 establishes incident preparation frameworks including communication channels and notification procedures.
NSL § 5-2 obligation to protect classified information is operationally implemented through NSM GP 2.7 data protection controls for data at rest and in transit.
NSL § 5-3 security classification levels provide a Norwegian government-specific data classification framework. NIS2 risk management mandates data classification as a risk management measure.
NSL § 5-3 security classification levels (STRENGT HEMMELIG, HEMMELIG, KONFIDENSIELT, BEGRENSET) map to ISO 27001 Annex A information classification and labeling controls.
NSL § 5-4 need-to-know principle and access restrictions for classified information are operationally implemented through NSM GP 2.6 identity and access control measures.
NSL § 5-6 Kryptosikkerhet (cryptographic security) requirements support NSM GP 2.7 data protection principles, particularly encryption of data at rest and in transit.
NSL Chapter 6 information system security requirements are implemented through NSM GP 2.2 secure ICT architecture principles, including segmentation and isolation for classified systems.
NSL § 6-2 protection of classified information systems includes secure configuration requirements, implemented through NSM GP 2.3 secure configuration controls.
NSL § 6-4 mandatory monitoring of classified information systems is implemented through NSM GP 3.2 security monitoring establishment principles.
NSL § 6-5 penetration testing requirement for classified systems is implemented through NSM GP 3.4 penetration testing principles.

NSL § 7-3 protection requirements for classified objects and infrastructure map to NIST SP 800-53 Physical and Environmental Protection (PE) family, both requiring proportionate physical security measures.
NSL Chapter 8 Personellsikkerhet maps to NIST SP 800-53 Personnel Security (PS) family. Both establish screening requirements, clearance procedures, and personnel termination controls.

NSL Chapter 8 Personellsikkerhet maps to NIS2 human resources security and access control requirements. NSL provides the Norwegian government security vetting framework addressing NIS2 personnel security obligations.
NSL § 8-9 Autorisasjon formalizes the access grant for cleared personnel, complementing NSM GP 2.6 identity and access control lifecycle management.
NSL Chapter 9 security-graded procurement adds legal clearance obligations to NSM GP 2.1 security in acquisition and development processes.
NSL Chapter 9 security-graded procurement maps to NIS2 supply chain security requirement. Both mandate that suppliers meet appropriate security standards when handling sensitive information.
NSL § 9-2 Sikkerhetsavtale med leverandør implements ISO 27001 Annex A supplier relationship controls, specifically the requirement for security agreements in contracts.
NSL § 9-1 facility security clearance for businesses maps to NIST SP 800-53 Supply Chain Risk Management (SR) family controls for supplier vetting and qualification.

NSL § 1-2 scope definition (national security-significant entities) and NIS2 governance (essential/important entities) address overlapping but distinct entity populations subject to security obligations.
